iti109:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti109:2.1: “Monks, suppose a man were being carried along by the flow of a river, lovely & alluring.
iti109:2.2: And then another man with good eyesight, standing on the bank, on seeing him would say,
iti109:2.3:
‘My good man, even though you are being carried along by the flow of a river, lovely & alluring,
further down from here is a pool with waves & whirlpools, with seizers & demons.
On reaching that pool you will suffer death or death-like pain.’
iti109:2.4:
Then the first man, on hearing the words of the second man,
would make an effort with his hands & feet to go against the flow.
iti109:3.1: “I have given you this simile to illustrate a meaning.
iti109:3.3: the flow of the river stands for craving.
iti109:4.1: Lovely & alluring stands for the six internal sense-media.
iti109:5.1: The pool further down stands for the five lower fetters.
iti109:6.1: The waves stand for anger & distress.
iti109:7.1: The whirlpools stand for the five strings of sensuality.
iti109:8.1: The seizers & demons stand for the opposite sex.
iti109:9.1: Against the flow stands for renunciation.
iti109:10.1: Making an effort with hands & feet stands for the arousing of persistence.
iti109:11.1: The man with good eyesight standing on the bank stands for the Tathāgata, worthy & rightly self-awakened.”
iti109:12.1: Even if it’s with pain, you should abandon sensual desires
iti109:12.2: if you aspire to future safety from bondage.
iti109:12.3: Rightly discerning, with a mind well released,
iti109:12.4: touch release now here, now there.
iti109:12.5: An attainer-of-wisdom, having fulfilled the holy life,
iti109:12.6: is said to have gone to the end of the world, gone beyond.
